_meta.placeholder_notice: All numbers PLACEHOLDER (R14). deal_sheet_hints values are direct 1-5 restatements of the entity personality axes (mythology-research-spec §5), not tuned constants.
_meta.supersession_note: SUPERSEDES the slice stubs in data/patron_gods.json LATER, not yet: patron_gods.json ids 1-5 stay in place because data/demo_loadouts.json still references chosen_patron ids 2 (enyo) and 3 (hestia). The migration re-points the demo loadouts first.

deal_sheet_hint_semantics.boon_frequency/boon_magnitude: generosity — boon frequency & magnitude; gift chance at affection thresholds
deal_sheet_hint_semantics.taboo_count: observed count of taboos on the deal sheet (strictness-correlated)
deal_sheet_hint_semantics.rival_curse_chance: pettiness — rival-curse chance when courting other gods
deal_sheet_hint_semantics.punishment_speed: wrath — punishment escalation speed after a taboo breach
deal_sheet_hint_semantics.buyout_resistance: fidelity — buy-out resistance (low = sells your contract sooner)

roster.name: Ganesha
roster.temperament: The floor's beloved first-bet concierge — every new player's chips pass under his trunk for luck before touching a table, and the house never opens a season without him.
favor_conditions: perform an offering/salute action at the run's first threshold before any combat
clear a blocking obstacle (barricade, rubble, locked route) instead of routing around it
learn a boss's win condition before dealing it any damage
sacrifice a piece of your own equipped gear to complete an objective (the broken-tusk clause)
taboos: mock a fallen contestant's failure on camera (the moon clause)
skip an available threshold offering at a run's start
let food supplies spoil or go to waste
roster.blessing_style: obstacles quietly resolve ahead of you — locked doors found open, traps pre-sprung; boons front-load run starts and the first attempt of anything
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Avalokiteshvara
roster.temperament: Runs the casino's only genuinely charitable foundation from a thousand help-desk windows at once — answers every call personally, forgives every debt, and has never once left the phones.
favor_conditions: revive, stabilize, or carry a downed teammate before advancing the objective
call for help aloud (use a call/signal action) when below 25% HP instead of dying silently
accept an enemy's surrender and let it leave the arena alive on camera
take a hit intended for another contestant (intercept/guard action)
taboos: execute a helpless or surrendering enemy
abandon a rescuable teammate or NPC to save run time
roster.blessing_style: a thousand small mercies — the ledge you barely catch, the blade that misses by a finger; heals arrive at the exact moment a cry for help is spoken aloud
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Amaterasu
roster.temperament: Chairwoman-emeritus of the eight-million-kami Takamagahara talent agency; one on-air outrage from her brother and she'll cancel every broadcast in existence — her staff keep a mirror, a comedian, and a strongman on retainer for the next blackout.
favor_conditions: Clear a dungeon floor engaging enemies only in lit tiles or after deploying a light source — never fight in darkness
Recover the crowd from a hype-meter floor to a cheer within the same fight (stage a comeback the audience laughs or roars at)
Keep every promise sworn on camera for an entire run (declared pledges tracked, zero breaches)
Lure a fortified boss out of its lair instead of storming in — make the enemy come to you
taboos: Defile a camp, shrine, or rest zone (leave corpses or filth where allies eat and sleep)
Break an oath sworn on camera
Abandon an ally alone in darkness
roster.blessing_style: Daylight banking: steady boons that compound while conduct stays clean — and a total blackout of favor (absence, not punishment) the moment the deal-sheet is dirtied; her light returns only after a genuine crowd-winning comeback.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Yama
roster.temperament: The house auditor: reads your ledger back to you line by line, sighs, stamps it — and privately dreams of quitting the office to play the tables as a mortal.
favor_conditions: report your kill count and loot exactly at every end-of-floor tally (no inflation, no omission)
read every posted warning/messenger sign on a floor before engaging its boss
grant a mortally wounded enemy a clean finishing stroke instead of leaving it to bleed out
accept a rules penalty without contesting it when fairly caught
taboos: falsify the ledger — claim a kill or loot that is not yours
kill outside the game's sanctioned bounds (noncombatant NPCs)
roster.blessing_style: exact accounting — foreknowledge as boon: what a fight will cost you, itemized, before you commit to it
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Palden Lhamo
roster.temperament: Rides her mule between VVIP tables across a sea of blood, divination dice swinging from the saddle — the only regular who has already paid the worst price a bet can ask, and plays like it.
favor_conditions: accept a dice-shrine gamble (randomized loadout or route) before a boss and play out the result
publicly call your victory before the run begins and deliver it
sacrifice your highest-value carried item to keep a declared vow alive
finish a floor after losing your mount, vehicle, or movement gear
taboos: re-roll, cancel, or scum a gamble after seeing the result
abandon a sworn cause when the price turns personal
roster.blessing_style: the dice decide — boons come as brutal either/or wagers; take the roll as it lands and she rides beside you, hedge and she rides on
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Caishen
roster.temperament: The casino's market-maker — a New-Year rainmaker in a gold-trimmed suit who loves a bold position, remembers every ledger line, and will sell your contract the moment a better bid lands.
favor_conditions: open the run by wagering or offering your own currency at the table shrine
end the run with more currency than you entered with, after all fees
convert a boss drop into currency at the shop instead of equipping it
return another contestant's dropped loot bag intact on camera
taboos: be caught broke — reach zero currency on camera
wantonly destroy loot or currency
default on a debt owed to another contestant
roster.blessing_style: Compounding returns — small boons that multiply if reinvested rather than spent, with jackpot windfalls timed to festival broadcasts; his blessings always arrive as credit first.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Santa Muerte
roster.temperament: The saint the desperate pray to when every other patron has passed — generous to the outlaw and the orphan alike, and merciless to anyone who breaks a promise to her.
favor_conditions: take a champion flagged doomed / lowest-odds into a Forsaken game and survive
keep a vow made to her for the entire run (honor a self-imposed rule to the letter)
finish the strong and the weak enemies alike, sparing none for reward (show death no favoritism)
taboos: break a promise sworn to her on camera
court a rival patron while carrying her favor (she is the jealous saint)
roster.blessing_style: the impartial hand — boons come to anyone who asks sincerely, outlaw or saint; generous and unconditional up front, then withdrawn with terrifying speed the instant a vow is broken.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Eshu
roster.temperament: Valet, bouncer, switchboard, and rigger of every doorway in the building, in a suit red on one side and black on the other; tip him going in or your chips, your messages, and your luck all reach the wrong table — a liability the house settled by making him a partner.
favor_conditions: pay a toll at the run's first door — leave an item or currency at the entrance tile before engaging anything
at a fork, take the unscouted route and clear what you find there
turn two enemies against each other and let them fight before you strike
declare your next move on camera and land it exactly as called
taboos: enter a floor without paying or acknowledging the gatekeeper (skip the entrance offering)
claim on camera that luck played no part in your win
shortchange a promised offering after the boon has already landed
roster.blessing_style: coin-flip boons at doors and crossings — paid first, he swaps outcomes at thresholds (the ambush misfires, the wrong door is right); unpaid, the same swaps run against you
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Odin
roster.temperament: Plays like a hedge-fund doomer who has read the settlement date of his own bankruptcy — insider-trading against his own obituary and paying for tips in body parts.
favor_conditions: win by sacrificing something of real value mid-run (destroy or permanently give up an equipped item, stat, or skill) and convert the loss into the winning play
learn a boss's win condition before first engaging it (scout, interrogate, or read the arena) and defeat it that way
keep fighting and land a finishing blow while one of your body parts is disabled
interrogate or spare an enemy to extract intelligence, then win the encounter using it
taboos: break an oath sworn on camera
waste knowledge freely given — ignore an NPC's explicit warning and wipe on the thing it warned about
refuse a riddle or wisdom-contest offered at the table
roster.blessing_style: cryptic pre-run intelligence — boons front-loaded as information, not stats: map fragments, boss-weakness whispers, rune-marked doors; the price is always named after it has already been paid
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: The Morrígan
roster.temperament: Sits at the sportsbook window betting battle outcomes she already called — and takes it very personally when a contestant declines her tip.
favor_conditions: win an engagement you were explicitly warned (omen event / scout report) you would lose
defeat a named enemy champion in single combat while your party stands out of the fight
accept an unidentified offer of aid or a gift from an NPC mid-run without vetting it
finish a battle leaving no enemy unit alive to retreat
taboos: refuse aid, a gift, or affection offered by an NPC on camera
declare victory certain (boast emote / prediction) before the fight resolves
roster.blessing_style: Omens and battlefield prophecy — she marks who falls before the fight starts; boons arrive as enemy-fate intel and fear/rout pressure, and her displeasure literally fights beside your enemies in beast-shapes.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Morgan le Fay
roster.temperament: The one player here with real worshippers in the stands — a fae-queen VIP who heals with one hand and poisons your rival's chips with the other, all smiles for the camera and knives for the recap.
favor_conditions: win a duel you could have avoided — take the pettier, more personal path to victory
steal or turn an enemy's own weapon/relic against them and finish the run wielding it
heal or revive an ally at real cost to yourself, then still close the run
humiliate a rival's champion on camera before you beat them
taboos: show mercy to someone Morgan has marked, or refuse a vengeance she offers you
court her rival the Lady of the Lake in the same run — the two fay will not share a table
roster.blessing_style: two-faced fae sorcery — a healing or shape-shifting boon that also poisons a rival's luck; gifts come wrapped in a grudge, and the price is always someone else's ruin.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Benzaiten
roster.temperament: Headline lounge act and treasury director in one — she'll launder your winnings in holy water, double them, and curse your date on the way out.
favor_conditions: Perform mid-combat (music, dance, or flourish action) and hold a hype streak through a full fight
Wash or tithe currency at a water feature, then stake the washed sum the same day (the Zeniarai rite)
Talk an enemy or rival down instead of fighting — win an eloquence check on camera (she tamed the five-headed dragon with words)
Enter a boss arena solo
taboos: Flaunt a romance at her table (the Enoshima jealousy: she splits couples who parade before her)
Interrupt or talk over a performance
Pawn a musical instrument
roster.blessing_style: Flow-state artistry: multipliers that build while you keep performing and vanish the instant you play it safe — plus laundered luck: money staked after her water rite returns multiplied.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Zeus
roster.temperament: The chairman on permanent Hawaii vacation — floral shirt over the aegis, mai tai in the thunderbolt hand, still insisting every big wager on the floor needs his sign-off; it does, and his oaths have fine print.
favor_conditions: swear a public oath before the run and keep it to the letter through the final room
win a duel you accepted in front of the audience
strike the killing blow on a boss from high ground or during an active storm/lightning field
shelter a weaker contestant who asks for protection and finish the run with them alive
taboos: break any oath you swore on camera
harm a contestant who has formally surrendered or begged your protection
fake or claim credit for a god's intervention
roster.blessing_style: rare, loud, table-shaking — favor lands as single decisive thunder-strikes and public displays of endorsement, never quiet drip-feeds
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Athena
roster.temperament: Came to the pantheon's Hawaii vacation with sensible sandals and a laminated itinerary; sits behind the high-rollers running the odds in her head and never touches the dice — she bets on preparation, not luck.
favor_conditions: identify a boss's win condition before first contact with it
clear a combat encounter without taking a single point of damage
defeat a stronger enemy using terrain, a trap, or a prepared ambush
craft or upgrade a piece of equipment mid-run and win a fight with it
taboos: boast on camera that your skill needed no god's help
desecrate a shrine or sanctuary tile
engage a boss with zero scouting information
roster.blessing_style: front-loaded intelligence — map fragments, enemy tells, a whispered plan; her multipliers pay out on flawless execution of a prepared play
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Hades
roster.temperament: The house that always collects — never gambles, never vacations; sends a corporate regrets card to the beach party and quietly buys the hotel.
favor_conditions: recover a fallen teammate's remains or token and carry it to the exit
finish a run without using any revival or second-chance item
collect every coin and treasure drop on a floor before leaving it
hold an underground or lowest-level room against a wave without retreating
taboos: use any death-cheating trick — revives, resurrection items, feigned death
renege on the stated terms of any deal made at the table
leave your own dead unburied on camera
roster.blessing_style: silent compounding interest — boons accrue invisibly and pay out at the death-door moment; his favor is never seen until it is decisive
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Hermes
roster.temperament: The floor runner who deals, delivers, and skims — invented half the games and the loaded dice the door checks for; runs a shave-ice stand out front strictly as a front.
favor_conditions: steal an item from an elite or boss without triggering combat
find and use a hidden shortcut or secret passage
talk or trade your way past an encounter designed to be fought
accept any wager offered mid-run and play it out
taboos: harm a herald, courier, or merchant NPC
walk the marked main path from entrance to exit with zero detours
leave a discovered windfall (hermaion) unclaimed
roster.blessing_style: opportunist's luck — doors left unlocked, guards distracted, dice quietly weighted; boons arrive as absurdly convenient timing
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Loki
roster.temperament: The card-counter the pit bosses can't ban because he co-founded the casino — will bet his own head, lawyer the payout down to 'the head, not the neck,' and take his lips sewn shut before admitting the L.
favor_conditions: win an encounter without dealing damage — stealth, deception, or positioning resolves it
talk or trick your way past a guardian on camera
exploit a rule's or reward's literal wording in a way the audience can see
steal something from a boss and leave the fight alive
taboos: play an encounter straight when a visible cheat was available
repeat the same winning tactic three encounters running — boring his audience
roster.blessing_style: loopholes made flesh — rerolls, identity swaps, one glorious rules-inversion per run; every gift is refurbished, every receipt is forged, and the warranty voids at the worst possible moment
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Tezcatlipoca
roster.temperament: The corporate raider who holds short positions on every contestant including his own — he hands you the drink, films what happens, and calls it market research.
favor_conditions: turn a rival contestant's own trap, weapon, or summon against them
win a fight in darkness or while blinded
completely change loadout or build between floors and still win the next fight
publicly humiliate a stronger contestant without dealing a single point of damage
taboos: repeat the same winning tactic on consecutive floors (he despises predictability)
thank or credit another god on camera while contracted to him
roster.blessing_style: A reversal-of-fortune engine — multipliers spike when your odds are worst; he will curse his own champion mid-run purely to improve the show, then pay triple if you adapt.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Inanna
roster.temperament: The founding family's ambitious heiress — walks into any boardroom wearing whatever she took from the last one, and has already sat in your chair.
favor_conditions: Win a fight the odds board flagged as beyond your rating
Take a boss's or rival contestant's signature equipment and wear or use it on camera in the same run
Return to a room where you were previously downed and clear it
Hold the run's highest single-encounter hype/camera-call gain
taboos: Decline a boon, gift, or advance she offers (the Gilgamesh precedent — refusal cost Uruk a Bull)
Credit another god on camera for a kill her boon powered
roster.blessing_style: Spotlight escalation — comps arrive as finery and weapons lifted from other gods' tables and grow with your hype; all-or-nothing multipliers when you fight above your class.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Beelzebub
roster.temperament: Old money gone rancid: a demoted god who still throws around a fortune that smells of decay, buzzing with a retinue that eats what it touches.
favor_conditions: win an encounter by attrition — let poison or infection do the finishing work
turn an enemy's own numbers against it (make a swarm or summon kill its master)
hold a chokepoint for a set number of turns without yielding ground
taboos: cleanse or cure a condition you inflicted (waste the rot)
keep a clean, tidy killbox — refuse to let the field degrade
roster.blessing_style: spread — boons are contagions: buffs that multiply across a swarm and creep onto whatever you touch, strongest in a filthy, condition-soaked arena.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Gad
roster.temperament: The oldest patron in the room and the most literal â€” the god you set a table for before the word 'casino' existed, who deals in nothing but the tilt of the odds.
favor_conditions: win an encounter decided by a coin-flip / low-odds outcome (let the gamble land)
set an offering at a shrine or table before a run and then complete the run
take the riskier of two offered paths and clear it
taboos: hedge every bet â€” never take an offered high-risk/high-reward option across a whole run
curse your luck aloud after a bad outcome (blaspheme against fortune)
roster.blessing_style: pure fortune â€” boons are reweighted dice: Gad doesn't make you stronger, he makes the improbable break your way; the casino's oldest and most literal patron.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Lucifer
roster.temperament: The floor's most famous degenerate — went all-in against the house once, lost everything, and has been working the comeback ever since, grinning like the pot is already his.
favor_conditions: decline the safe reward and take the higher-stakes option at a fork
win a Forsaken (VVIP) game solo after refusing all mid-run help
beat an enemy that outclasses you on paper (win while flagged the underdog)
taboos: accept a buy-out from the Firm or a messenger after signing with him
cash out early / win small instead of pushing the pot
roster.blessing_style: leverage — boons are loans against your future: enormous power now, a debt that compounds; he front-loads the comeback and lets you carry the risk.
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Mammon
roster.temperament: The deepest pockets on the floor and the shallowest loyalty — backs whoever's up, folds on whoever's down, and calls it good business.
favor_conditions: end a run with the largest treasure haul on the board (top the greed leaderboard)
buy your way past an obstacle instead of fighting it (spend, don't sweat)
win a bidding war for a contested reward against a rival
taboos: give away loot for free (hand a reward to an ally without payment)
leave a treasure uncollected when you could have taken it
roster.blessing_style: liquidity — boons are purchasable and instant, the deepest pockets on the floor; but he backs only whoever's winning and drops a losing book without sentiment (buy-out magnet — lowest fidelity on the roster).
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.

roster.name: Anansi
roster.temperament: Eight hands, one credit line, and a royalty on everything ever broadcast — he sold the house its own story catalog after winning it in the wildest all-in on record (stake: his own mother). Bet him nothing you can't afford to become a story.
favor_conditions: beat a foe or obstacle that outclasses you using trickery, a trap, or the terrain — not raw force (the small outwitting the mighty)
complete an objective by an unsanctioned method nobody proposed or expected
win an on-camera wager or bargain and collect on it to the letter
accept an 'impossible' task and clear it through a clever workaround (his four-capture price to Nyame)
taboos: win by brute force when a clever option was on the table — Anansi has no patience for the graceless
renege on a bargain or a bet you struck
roster.blessing_style: the trickster's edge — boons are workarounds and reversals: turn a stronger foe's own strength against it, slip a trap, or flip a losing bet; small, cheap, and always a little bit of a cheat
roster.notes: PLACEHOLDER numbers (R14). Deal sheet derived from entities.jsonl patron_block; hints restate personality axes per mythology-research-spec §5.
